items.title: Deutsche Bank cuts Q3 gold price target by 22%
items.snippet: Deutsche Bank has become the latest major institution to revise down its near-term target for gold prices amid concerns over inflation and high interest rates. In a note published Tuesday, the German investment bank said it now sees gold averaging $4,300/oz. in the third quarter of this year, which is 22% lower than its previous forecast. items.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Post Another Major Draw
items.snippet: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.1 million barrels during the week ending June 19, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 412.1 million barrels, according to government data, which is now 7% below the five-year average for this time of year. The EIA’s data release follo

items.title: UK Climate Panel Urges Faster Electrification to Lower Energy Bills
items.snippet: UK’s slower-than-expected electrification exposes households to fossil fuel price shocks, the country’s Climate Change Committee (CCC) said on Wednesday, urging faster electrification and removal of policy costs to lower the energy bills. items.title: Euro Sinks To One-Year Low As Oil Price Drop Fuels ECB Rate Cut Bets
items.snippet: The euro has sunk to a one-year low, with easing of tensions between the United States and Iran helping to cool oil prices and raise expectations that the European Central Bank will turn more dovish.
